title: rubus neglectus
intro: Rubus neglectus, also known as the woodland bramble or neglected raspberry, is a wild plant species that belongs to the rose family. The medicinal properties of Rubus neglectus include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and dietary fiber, providing several health benefits such as improving digestion, boosting the immune system, and reducing inflammation. Rubus neglectus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat ailments like fever, wounds, and digestive problems.
